title: toxicodendron trichocarpum
intro: Toxicodendron trichocarpum, also known as poison sumac, is a flowering plant native to North America, characterized by its white or greenish-white berries and reddish stems, and is highly toxic due to the presence of urushiol oil. The medicinal properties of poison sumac include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. The health benefits of poison sumac are largely anecdotal, but it is said to have antiseptic and antifungal properties, which can aid in wound healing and skin infections. Poison sumac can be prepared into herbal teas, salves, and infusions, but caution is advised due to its highly toxic nature, as it requires proper handling and preparation to avoid exposure to urushiol oil.
